title: Astracantha dschuparensis
intro: Astracantha dschuparensis, also known as Dschuparen's milkvetch, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Astracantha dschuparensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, as well as being used in the production of herbal tinctures and extracts.
taxonomy: Astracantha dschuparensis is a species of plant in the genus Astracantha, which is a group of plants within the legume family (Fabaceae). The genus Astracantha is a part of the larger subfamily Faboideae. Within this subfamily, Astracantha dschuparensis is further classified as a member of the tribe Galegeae. This tribe consists of plants with a specific type of flower and seed arrangement. Astracantha dschuparensis is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia.
